input.headline: Kids do not want to go to child-free weddings either

raw.headline: Kids do not want to go to child-free weddings either

winner: A kid, overhearing his parents' conversation about a child-free wedding, asks, "So, no kids allowed at the 'wedding without wedgies'?

candidates.text: A kid overheard, "They're having a child-free wedding?" He replied, "Guess they want the reception to be 'grown-ups only'!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the phrase 'grown-ups only,' concise and specific to the headline topic."}

candidates.text: A kid, overhearing his parents' conversation about a child-free wedding, asks, "So, no kids allowed at the 'wedding without wedgies'?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'wedgies' and 'child-free,' concise and surprising with a specific scenario."}

candidates.text: Why did the child refuse to attend the pet-free circus? Because he heard there would be no elephants!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks direct connection and misses the mark on specificity and surprise."}

candidates.text: A wedding without kids, and not even a sandbox for the adults? No wonder the kids are turning down that offer!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the twist of implying kids are also turned off by child-free weddings, but it's a bit forced and lacks punch."}

candidates.text: Well, at least they're consistent. They didn't want to go to school either.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and concise, but the connection between not wanting to go to weddings and school feels a bit forced and lacks specificity."}
